charity.s: $3M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.01% of revenue).
charity.focus: Essential needs for low-income families, youth development, military family support

guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

wiki.extract: Family Dollar Stores, Inc. is an American variety store chain founded in 1959 by Leon Levine in Charlotte, North Carolina. With over 8,000 locations in all states except Alaska and Hawaii, it was once the second largest retailer of its type in the United States until it was acquired by Dollar Tree in 2015. Its headquarters operations were then moved from Matthews, North Carolina, to Chesapeake, Virginia.

cases.caseName: DUNN v. FAMILY DOLLAR STORES OF FLORIDA LLC
cases.natureOfSuit: 751 Labor: Family and Medical Leave Act

health.s: 17 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: the recalled loungers can collapse unexpectedly.

topHazards.label: The recalled loungers can collapse unexpectedly, posing an injury hazard if fingers get caught in the metal folding joints.
The wax warmers can produce high flames, posing fire and burn hazards to consumers.
The die-cast metal cars can have sharp edges that pose a laceration hazard.
The heaters can overheat posing a fire hazard.
The light sets do not meet the UL standard for this product and pose a fire and shock risk.

sampleRecalls.title: Family Dollar Recalls Beach Loungers Due to Injury Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves beach lounge chairs with a white metal frame and either red or blue woven plastic fabric. The lounge chairs have a hang tag intended to be removed after purchase stating "Outdoors by Design" on one side and "Distributed by Midwoods Brands LLC" on the other side, along with either SKU 3499631 or SK…
hazards: The recalled loungers can collapse unexpectedly, posing an injury hazard if fingers get caught in the metal folding joints.

sampleRecalls.title: Family Dollar Recalls Wax Warmers Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves ceramic wax warmer sets that include four vanilla-scented wax cubes for melting and a teacup-shaped ceramic warmer with an opening to insert a tea light candle. Candles are not sold as part of set. The ceramic warmers were colored cream with gold trim, and green with gold trim.
hazards: The wax warmers can produce high flames, posing fire and burn hazards to consumers.
injuries: Family Dollar has received 11 reports of the warmers producing high flames, including two reports of burn injuries and three reports of property damage.

sampleRecalls.title: Family Dollar Stores Recall Tough Treadz Auto Carrier Toy Sets Due to Laceration Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves a plastic toy truck with a plastic case that holds 6 die-cast metal toy cars in assorted colors. The truck is 14 inches long x 3 inches wide x 5 inches high. The cab of the truck comes in black, blue or red. The package is labeled as "Tough Treadz Auto Carrier" and has a white sticker in the upper…
hazards: The die-cast metal cars can have sharp edges that pose a laceration hazard.

sampleRecalls.title: Family Dollar Stores Recalls Optimus Tower Quartz Heaters Due to Overheating and Fire Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: Optimus Tower Quartz Heaters are portable electric tower heaters that are about 10 inches wide, 25 inches tall and 9 inches deep. The heaters have a white metal casing with a white plastic top, a wire cage protecting the heating elements and vent slits at the bottom. The front section of the top has the brand name Opt…
hazards: The heaters can overheat posing a fire hazard.
injuries: Family Dollar Stores has received 10 reports of overheating, including some reports of temperature knobs melting. The firm has not received reports of injury, fire or property damage.

sampleRecalls.title: Family Dollar Stores Recalls Decorative Light Sets Due To Fire and Electrical Shock Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled decorative light sets have "200 Mini Lights" and "Multi Lights, Green Wire" printed on the red box. The product is identified by SKU # 2211428 and UPC # 049696720465 found on the back of the package. The product contains labels attached to a tag on the power cord with UL Listing # E346525 and Model # F0L2…
hazards: The light sets do not meet the UL standard for this product and pose a fire and shock risk.
injuries: There were three reports of overheating. No injuries or property damage were reported.

sampleRecords.event_description: Hitting, kicking, beating by other person  n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was attempting to stop two shoplifters when one of the shoplifters pushed her to the floor. The back of her head was injured after it struck the floor and the employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle backing up in nonroadway area
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking to their car in the parking lot while on break when they were struck by a car that was backing up. The employee fell to the ground and suffered a head injury.
An employee exited the store to obtain a person's license plate number. The person then brandished a firearm and shot the employee in the leg.

sampleRecords.nature: Cuts, lacerations, punctures without injury to internal structures
sampleRecords.event_description: Stabbing, cutting, slashing by other person
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was stabbed with a knife several times in the torso by a shoplifter. The employee was hospitalized.
An employee was leaving a store after closing it for the evening. The employee was shot five times in a drive-by shooting.
